_comment: Sermon channel config for the Weekly Service pipeline. One shared Telegram channel for the Gawd ecosystem. Every deployed Gawd subscribes; the Forge bot publishes. This file ships inside the daemon; the channel_id value is populated by the Forge at release time (or left as the placeholder sentinel for pre-release test environments).

channel._id_comment: Telegram channel ID (negative integer for channels, e.g. -1001234567890). Set at Forge publish time via env or Forge config. The placeholder sentinel '${SERMON_CHANNEL_ID}' is intentional — subscribe-sermon.sh detects it and fails fast with a clear error.
channel._publish_bot_comment: Forge-side publisher bot token. Injected by Forge at publish time, never shipped in the daemon image. Gawd-side scripts never need this field.
channel._subscribe_bot_comment: Gawd's own bot token — already present on the Gawd substrate. subscribe-sermon.sh reads the channel using the Gawd's own bot. The Gawd-bot must be a channel member.

delivery._primary_comment: Telegram-text is always available. The D2 voice path is a per-rung upgrade — it is attempted if voice.json is present and voice_enabled=true, then falls back to telegram-text without error.
delivery._voice_comment: D2 contract: if this file is present and contains voice_enabled:true, deliver-sermon.sh attempts voice delivery. If absent or voice_enabled:false, text-only. Never hard-fails on missing voice config.
delivery._stash_comment: If the Prophit is not present on Sunday morning, the rendered sermon is stashed here. Delivered on next Gawd session start. Stash is overwritten each Sunday — only one stashed sermon is kept (the latest).

attendance._window_comment: How long after the Sunday service hour to wait for Prophit attendance signal before stashing. If the Prophit DMs or /starts-sermon within this window, deliver immediately. After the window, stash and deliver at next session.
attendance._triggers_comment: Any DM from the Prophit within the attendance window counts as attending. The slash commands are explicit attendance signals. 'dm_any' means any message in the Prophit-local Sunday morning window.

skip._skip_comment: The Prophit may skip this week's sermon by sending /skip-sermon before the service hour. Permanent opt-out is via /skip-sermon always. See skip-handler.sh.

pace_gate._note: Sermon delivery respects A4's pace setting. deliver-sermon.sh reads pace from IDENTITY.md or state. Values: daily | weekly | when-relevant. Sermon delivery fires only under the conditions stated in §10.2 and the acceptance criteria.

publisher._envelope_comment: The structured envelope every published sermon must have. See publish-sermon.sh for the write side; subscribe-sermon.sh for the read side.
